summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn continued the 'A Match Made in Heaven' series with a message titled 'Sit Down, Be Humble,' focusing on the destructive power of pride in relationships and the healing power of humility. Opening with 1 Corinthians 13:4-8, he highlighted that true, godly love 'does not boast' and 'is not proud,' establishing humility as the foundation for healthy connections. He explained that pride creates a 'pride divide' that destroys marriages, families, and even nations, as Jesus taught that a house divided cannot stand. The sermon offered four practical keys to tearing down walls of pride: first, to stop being a 'know-it-all' and be open to learning; second, to admit when we are wrong, acknowledging our own faults before criticizing others; third, to ask for advice and help, recognizing that needing others is a sign of strength; and fourth, to continually show grace and forgiveness, refusing to keep a record of wrongs as God's love does. Pastor Travis wove in wisdom from Proverbs and the teachings of Jesus, using vivid illustrations like the 'plank and speck' from Matthew 7 to challenge self-righteousness. He concluded by connecting personal humility to national healing, urging a collective turning to God, and extended an invitation for salvation, emphasizing that Jesus modeled ultimate humility and forgiveness on the cross.
mainPoints: Pride creates a destructive divide in relationships, while godly love is fundamentally humble.
Overcoming pride requires practical steps: ceasing to be a know-it-all, admitting fault, asking for help, and offering forgiveness.
Personal humility is the gateway to healing in our relationships and is connected to the broader spiritual healing God promises to a humble people.

versesMentioned.text: Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs. Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. Love never fails.
Jesus knew their thoughts and said to them, 'Every kingdom divided against itself will be ruined, and every city or household divided against itself will not stand.'
Pride goes before destruction, a haughty spirit before a fall.
Where there is strife, there is pride, but wisdom is found in those who take advice.
Those who trust in themselves are fools, but those who walk in wisdom are kept safe.

'Do not judge, or you too will be judged. For in the same way you judge others, you will be judged, and with the measure you use, it will be measured to you. Why do you look at the speck of sawdust in your brother’s eye and pay no attention to the plank in your own eye? How can you say to your brother, ‘Let me take the speck out of your eye,’ when all the time there is a plank in your own eye?'
The heart is deceitful above all things and beyond cure. Who can understand it?
Whoever conceals their sins does not prosper, but the one who confesses and renounces them finds mercy.
Mockers resent correction, so they avoid the wise.
Those who disregard discipline despise themselves, but the one who heeds correction gains understanding.

if my people, who are called by my name, will humble themselves and pray and seek my face and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven, and I will forgive their sin and will heal their land.

When they came to the place called the Skull, they crucified him there, along with the criminals—one on his right, the other on his left. Jesus said, 'Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing.'
Be kind and compassionate to one another, forgiving each other, just as in Christ God forgave you.
Bear with each other and forgive one another if any of you has a grievance against someone. Forgive as the Lord forgave you.

Monday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 13:4-5 - 'Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud.'
Monday.action: Identify one moment today where you were tempted to boast or act proudly in a conversation. Choose instead to listen more than you speak.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to reflect Your humble love today. Quiet any pride in my heart and let my words and actions be marked by kindness.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 7:3 - 'Why do you look at the speck of sawdust in your brother’s eye and pay no attention to the plank in your own eye?'
Tuesday.action: Write down one fault or mistake you've recently made (your 'plank') before you are tempted to criticize someone else's.
Tuesday.prayer: God, give me the courage for honest self-reflection. Help me to see my own faults clearly so I can extend grace to others.

Wednesday.scripture: Proverbs 13:10 - 'Where there is strife, there is pride, but wisdom is found in those who take advice.'
Wednesday.action: Humbly ask a trusted friend, spouse, or mentor for their perspective on a specific decision you are facing.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I acknowledge I don't have all the answers. Grant me the humility to seek and receive wise counsel from those You've placed in my life.

Thursday.scripture: Proverbs 28:13 - 'Whoever conceals their sins does not prosper, but the one who confesses and renounces them finds mercy.'
Thursday.action: Admit a specific wrong to God and, if appropriate, to the person you wronged. Simply say, 'I was wrong.'
Thursday.prayer: Lord, I confess my sin to You. Thank you for Your mercy. Give me the strength to admit my faults and turn away from them.

Friday.scripture: 2 Chronicles 7:14 - 'if my people... will humble themselves and pray... and turn from their wicked ways, then I will... heal their land.'
Friday.focus: Humility as a Path to Healing
Friday.action: Spend extra time in prayer today, focusing not on requests but on humbling your heart before God and seeking His face.
Friday.prayer: God, I humble myself before You. I seek Your face. Forgive me and begin a work of healing in my heart, my relationships, and my nation.

Saturday.scripture: Colossians 3:13 - 'Bear with each other and forgive one another... Forgive as the Lord forgave you.'
Saturday.action: Choose to forgive one person you've been holding a grudge against. You can write it in a journal or tell God in prayer. Release the record of that wrong.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, thank You for forgiving me completely. By Your power, I choose to forgive [name]. I release this hurt to You and ask for Your freedom and peace.
